name: External Remote Services
description: Adversaries leverage external-facing remote services such as VPNs, Citrix, Azure Bastion, Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD), or Direct Access to gain initial access or maintain persistence. In Azure/Entra ID environments, these services authenticate against Entra ID and generate SigninLogs entries. Adversaries with valid credentials may authenticate to Azure Bastion, AVD, or third-party VPN solutions integrated with Entra ID SAML/OIDC. Access through these services is particularly hard to distinguish from legitimate remote work traffic.
iocs: SigninLogs with AppDisplayName containing 'Azure Virtual Desktop', 'Windows Virtual Desktop', or 'Azure Bastion' from an IP not in corporate VPN ranges
SigninLogs showing successful authentication to remote access app from a country the user has never authenticated from
AuditLogs Operation 'Add member to group' for a remote-access-enabled security group performed by an account that recently authenticated remotely
SigninLogs DeviceDetail.operatingSystem mismatch: user typically uses Windows but remote session shows Linux or macOS UserAgent
Multiple concurrent sessions to Azure Virtual Desktop for the same UserPrincipalName from different IPAddress values — session sharing or token theft
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs showing refresh token usage for AVD app from a new IP immediately after interactive login from a different IP
SigninLogs ConditionalAccessStatus == 'notApplied' for remote service applications that should have a Conditional Access policy
evasion_variants: Use a residential proxy in the victim's home country to match expected geographic location
Authenticate during the user's normal business hours based on their historical SigninLogs timezone pattern
Use a compliant device claim via Intune MDM enrollment of an attacker-controlled device to satisfy device compliance CA policies
Leverage a personal access token or app password configured before MFA was enforced on the remote service application
Authenticate slowly over multiple days to avoid anomaly detection on new-IP sign-in alerts
Use the same UserAgent string as the legitimate user's most recent successful session
detection_kql_hints: SigninLogs | where AppDisplayName has_any ('Azure Virtual Desktop','Windows Virtual Desktop','Azure Bastion') | summarize dcount(IPAddress) by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h) | where dcount_IPAddress > 2
SigninLogs | where ResultType == 0 | extend Country = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion) | summarize make_set(Country) by UserPrincipalName | where array_length(set_Country) > 3
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs | where AppDisplayName has 'Virtual Desktop' | where IPAddress != prev(IPAddress) | where UserPrincipalName == prev(UserPrincipalName)
SigninLogs | where ConditionalAccessStatus == 'notApplied' and AppDisplayName has_any ('VPN','Bastion','Virtual Desktop') | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, AppDisplayName

Detect concurrent sessions: SigninLogs | where AppDisplayName has 'Virtual Desktop' | summarize Sessions=dcount(CorrelationId), IPs=dcount(IPAddress) by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m) | where IPs > 1
